description: Atari Games' 1996 prototype compilation featuring classic arcade titles.
Sega's 1981 space shooter with a unique fuel management mechanic. Destroy alien waves before running out of fuel.
Data East's 1988 beat-em-up classic. Are you a bad enough dude to rescue the president from the ninjas?
Atari's 1980 classic. Shoot a centipede winding through a mushroom field while fending off spiders and fleas.
Classic run-and-gun action by Konami. Fight through jungles and alien bases in this legendary two-player shooter.
Williams Electronics' landmark 1981 space shooter. Protect humanoids from alien abduction across a scrolling landscape.
Namco's classic tunnel-digging game. Inflate underground monsters with an air pump or crush them with falling rocks.
Iconic platformer by Nintendo. Climb ladders and dodge barrels thrown by Donkey Kong to rescue Pauline.
Legendary beat-em-up by Technos. Billy and Jimmy Lee battle through the streets to rescue Marian.
Capcom's beat-em-up classic. Mayor Mike Haggar, Cody, and Guy battle through Metro City to rescue Jessica from Mad Gear.
Classic fixed-shooter by Namco. Defend against waves of alien insects in one of the most iconic arcade games ever made.
Capcom's 1985 vertically scrolling western shooter. Blast bandits and bounty targets across the Wild West.
SNK's top-down military shooter. Battle through jungles and enemy fortresses with tanks and helicopters.
Williams' unique 1981 maze game. Paint the floor of the maze while avoiding enemies. Also known as Crush Roller.
Capcom's 1995 arcade fighter. Play as Mega Man, Proto Man, or Bass and defeat Dr. Wily's robot masters.
SNK's run-and-gun classic featuring the legendary Slug vehicles. The sixth entry in the acclaimed series.
The beloved sequel to Pac-Man. Navigate mazes eating dots and fruits while avoiding colorful ghosts.
Taito's 1987 fantasy hack-and-slash platformer. Play as Rastan, a barbarian warrior fighting through mythical lands.
Taito's 1992 wrestling arcade game featuring over-the-top moves and colorful fighters.
Capcom's prequel to Street Fighter II featuring a younger cast and the Alpha Counter system. Known as Street Fighter Zero in Japan.
The arcade sequel to Contra. Features both side-scrolling and top-down stages.
Nintendo's fast-paced boxing game. Fight through circuits of increasingly tough opponents.
SNK's 1986 vertical shooter. Pilot your transforming spacecraft through waves of enemy ships.
Sega's pioneering 1982 isometric space shooter. Fly through fortress defenses and defeat the robot Zaxxon.
